# Run Lifecycle

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/terminalresultmapping -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

Executable SSOT: [`run-lifecycle.riido.json`](run-lifecycle.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `terminal-result-mapping-evidence`.

## Terminal Result Mapping

| ResultStatus | EventType |
| --- | --- |
| `completed` | `RunReportedDone` |
| `failed` | `TaskFailed` |
| `blocked` | `TaskFailed` |
| `aborted` | `TaskFailed` |
| `cancelled` | `TaskCancelled` |
| `timeout` | `TaskTimedOut` |

## Defaults

- Empty status normalizes to `ResultCompleted`.
- Unknown terminal statuses fall back to `TaskFailed`.

## Lifecycle

```text
RunStarted
   -> TextDelta / ReasoningDelta / ToolCallStarted / ToolCallFinished
   -> FileChanged / CommandStarted / CommandFinished / StatusUpdate
   -> UsageDelta / LogLine
   -> InputRequested -> ProvideInput -> continue
   -> ApprovalRequested -> ResolveApproval -> continue
   -> RunReportedDone
```

`RunReportedDone` only records provider self-reported completion.
Task completion still depends on validation after the terminal event.

## Assertions

- `every ResultStatus const is represented in this manifest`
- `empty terminal status normalizes to ResultCompleted before mapping`
- `failed, blocked, and aborted fail closed through the default TaskFailed event`
- `cancelled and timeout remain distinct terminal transition events`
- `RunReportedDone means provider self-reported completion, not task validation completion`
